events.text: Paul Hernandez, 79-year-old Hispanic male, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the Prostate. Clinical staging: T3aN1M1b, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C61. Stage IV castration-resistant prostate cancer with bone metastases.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated first-line ADT with ARPI: Leuprolide 22.5 mg IM q3mo (ADT) + Enzalutamide 160 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging on first-line ADT with ARPI.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 hot flashes. Venlafaxine 37.5 mg daily started. Counseled on lifestyle modifications.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Bone scan performed for restaging after first-line chemotherapy for CRPC.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line chemotherapy for CRPC.
Completed palliative bone radiation: Palliative radiation 30 Gy in 10 fractions to L3 vertebral body. Treatment delivered as planned.
Clinical visit for post-radiation assessment.
Initiated second-line PARP inhibitor for BRCA2-mutant CRPC: Olaparib 300 mg BID. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line PARP inhibitor for BRCA2-mutant CRPC.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line PARP inhibitor for BRCA2-mutant CRPC.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging on second-line PARP inhibitor for BRCA2-mutant CRPC.
Bone scan performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Bone scan performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Paul Hernandez
MRN: patient_01aed571a032
Date of Study: 2022-09-27

CLINICAL INDICATION: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Enlarged prostate measuring 4.7 cm. Bilateral pelvic lymphadenopathy, largest node 2.8 cm.

BONES: Multiple mixed lytic-sclerotic osseous lesions involving lumbar spine, pelvis, and bilateral proximal femora consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Widespread osseous metastatic disease.
2. Pelvic lymphadenopathy consistent with nodal metastases.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Paul Hernandez
MRN: patient_01aed571a032
Specimen ID: S2022-33706
Date of Procedure: 2022-10-02
Date of Report: 2022-10-04

SPECIMEN: Prostate, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.0 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PSA: Positive
NKX3.1: Positive
AR: Positive
ERG: Positive (TMPRSS2-ERG fusion likely)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Paul Hernandez - Prostate" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 5.0 x 3.5 x 2.5 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A11.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Paul Hernandez
MRN: patient_01aed571a032
Date: 2022-10-10
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Hernandez is a 79-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate, IV. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage IV castration-resistant prostate cancer with bone metastases. Pathology confirmed acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports fatigue and unintentional 10-lb weight loss over 3 months.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- type 2 diabetes mellitus
- chronic kidney disease stage 2

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- No prior surgeries

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 5 years ago)
- Alcohol: Rare alcohol use
- Occupation: Nurse (retired)
- Lives with alone

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with prostate cancer at age 68. Paternal uncle with lung cancer.

ALLERGIES: Codeine (nausea)

MEDICATIONS:
- Metformin 1000 mg BID

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild peripheral edema, abdominal bloating, joint pain, mild dyspnea on exertion. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 143/85, HR 88, RR 14, Temp 98.1F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 92.7 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 8.2, Hgb 11.6, Plt 340, Cr 0.9

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
79-year-old male with IV acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. initial oncology consultation.

1. Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend first-line ADT with ARPI
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Port placement scheduled
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Paul Hernandez
MRN: patient_01aed571a032
Date of Report: 2022-10-21
Specimen: Prostate (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 2.5 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA2 p.E1493fs (c.4478delA) - Frameshift (Exon 11) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R273C - Missense (Exon 8)
  AR Amplification - Amplification
  PTEN Loss - Homozygous deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.S1893R - VUS
  CDK12 p.Q1142H - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA2 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitor olaparib indicated for HRR-mutated mCRPC.
  - Consider platinum-based chemotherapy.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 466x coverage.
Tumor content: 23%.

Electronically signed by: Andrew Patel, MD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Urology

Patient: Paul Hernandez
MRN: patient_01aed571a032
Date: 2022-11-21
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Hernandez is a 79-year-old male with IV acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate who presents for follow-up on first-line adt with arpi. Patient is tolerating treatment reasonably well. Reports mild nausea but eating adequately.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: decreased appetite, abdominal bloating, intermittent headaches. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 141/72, HR 94, RR 19, Temp 98.0F, SpO2 99% on RA, Wt 71.6 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 5.7, Hgb 11.0, Plt 292, Cr 0.77

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
79-year-old male with IV acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI.

1. Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 - follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI
   - Treatment well tolerated, continue
   - Will check labs prior to next cycle
2. Symptom management
   - Neuropathy being monitored
3. Follow-up in 6 weeks

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Urology

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Paul Hernandez
MRN: patient_01aed571a032
Date of Study: 2024-07-08

CLINICAL INDICATION: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 10 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Enlarged prostate measuring 5.1 cm. Bilateral pelvic lymphadenopathy, largest node 2.9 cm.

BONES: Multiple mixed lytic-sclerotic osseous lesions involving thoracic and lumbar spine consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Widespread osseous metastatic disease.
2. Pelvic lymphadenopathy consistent with nodal metastases.
3. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

BONE SCAN REPORT

Patient: Paul Hernandez
MRN: patient_01aed571a032
Date of Study: 2024-09-16

CLINICAL INDICATION: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 2 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body bone scintigraphy performed following IV injection of 25 mCi Tc-99m MDP.

FINDINGS:
Multiple foci of increased radiotracer uptake in the pelvis and lumbar spine consistent with widespread osseous metastatic disease. Approximately 10 lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1. Widespread osseous metastatic disease.
2. Pelvic lymphadenopathy consistent with nodal metastases.
3. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology
